title: Old Grudges, New Year
prose: Four years in, the lines between houses have long since hardened into habit — the same corridor sneers, the same reflexive needling in Potions, the same students who've decided exactly what they think of {{house}} and aren't much interested in revising the opinion.
But four years is also long enough to have seen past a few of those lines, here and there, when it mattered. Not every rivalry stays a rivalry forever.

choices.label: Some grudges you're not ready to let go of.
You've grown past at least one of them.
